message:
test: add hooks and session test suites

hooks_test.go (287 new lines):
- Comprehensive test coverage for the internal hooks package covering
  hook registration, execution, lifecycle, and error handling

session_test.go (175 new lines):
- Test coverage for session management including creation, lookup,
  cleanup, and concurrent access patterns

Also sync beads issue tracker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2ay73","title":"Add process name fallback for agent detection","description":"## Summary
When pane title doesn't match known agent patterns, fall back to querying the foreground process name to detect agents.

## Context
- GitHub Issue #20 reports agent detection fails when pane title changes dynamically
- Shell prompts and tmux hooks can change pane titles
- Need fallback detection mechanism

## Requirements
1. Primary: Check pane title against agent patterns (existing behavior)
2. Fallback: Query foreground process name via tmux
3. Cache results to avoid repeated lookups
4. Support all known agents: claude, codex, gemini-cli, aider, cursor

## Technical Approach
tmux list-panes -F '#{pane_current_command}' or tmux display-message -p -t PANE '#{pane_current_command}'

Process name patterns to detect:
- claude, claude-code -> Claude Code
- codex -> Codex CLI  
- gemini -> Gemini CLI
- aider -> Aider
- cursor -> Cursor

## Files to Modify
- internal/agent/patterns.go - Add process name patterns
- internal/robot/probe.go - Add fallback detection logic

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Agent detected even when pane title is custom/dynamic
- [ ] No performance regression (cache process lookups)
- [ ] Existing title-based detection still works as primary
- [ ] Tests cover both title and process detection paths","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T05:02:29.215906059Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T05:07:30.081155970Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T05:07:30.081113260Z","close_reason":"Implemented process name fallback in internal/tmux/session.go","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2b0eb","title":"AccountRotator: Track account rotation history","description":"## Purpose
Track account rotation history per pane to enable analytics, debugging, and rate limit learning across sessions.

## Implementation Details

```go
// RotationHistory tracks all account rotations with persistence
type RotationHistory struct {
    dataDir   string
    history   map[string][]RotationRecord // pane -> records
    mu        sync.RWMutex
    logger    *slog.Logger
}

type RotationRecord struct {
    Timestamp       time.Time `json:"timestamp"`
    SessionPane     string    `json:"session_pane"`
    AgentType       string    `json:"agent_type"`
    Project         string    `json:"project"`
    PreviousAccount string    `json:"previous_account"`
    NewAccount      string    `json:"new_account"`
    TriggerPattern  string    `json:"trigger_pattern"`
    TimeSinceLast   time.Duration `json:"time_since_last,omitempty"`
}

// RecordRotation logs a rotation event
func (h *RotationHistory) RecordRotation(r RotationRecord) {
    h.mu.Lock()
    defer h.mu.Unlock()
    
    paneHistory := h.history[r.SessionPane]
    
    // Calculate time since last rotation for this pane
    if len(paneHistory) > 0 {
        last := paneHistory[len(paneHistory)-1]
        r.TimeSinceLast = r.Timestamp.Sub(last.Timestamp)
    }
    
    h.history[r.SessionPane] = append(paneHistory, r)
    
    h.logger.Info("rotation recorded",
        "session_pane", r.SessionPane,
        "agent_type", r.AgentType,
        "previous", r.PreviousAccount,
        "new", r.NewAccount,
        "time_since_last", r.TimeSinceLast,
        "total_rotations", len(h.history[r.SessionPane]))
}

// GetRotationStats returns statistics for analysis
func (h *RotationHistory) GetRotationStats(agentType string) RotationStats {
    h.mu.RLock()
    defer h.mu.RUnlock()
    
    var stats RotationStats
    for _, records := range h.history {
        for _, r := range records {
            if r.AgentType == agentType {
                stats.TotalRotations++
                stats.AvgTimeBetween += r.TimeSinceLast
                stats.AccountUsage[r.NewAccount]++
            }
        }
    }
    // ... calculate averages
    return stats
}

// SaveToFile persists history to .ntm/rotation_history.json
func (h *RotationHistory) SaveToFile() error {
    // Similar to ratelimit.RateLimitTracker.SaveToDir pattern
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log each rotation with full context
- Log time since last rotation for pattern analysis
- Log statistics on request (debug level)

## Integration with Existing Code
- Follow persistence pattern from internal/ratelimit/tracker.go
- Store in .ntm directory alongside rate_limits.json

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Track rotation records with timestamps
- [ ] Calculate time between rotations per pane
- [ ] Provide aggregate statistics per agent type
- [ ] Persist to disk in .ntm directory
- [ ] Load history on startup
- [ ] Thread-safe access with RWMutex
- [ ] Unit tests for stats calculations","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:01:53.149378960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T16:49:31.126986810Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2b0eb","depends_on_id":"bd-3d4i9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2b0eb","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2b35","title":"EPIC: Session Profiles & Templates System","description":"**IDEA #12 - REDUCES DECISION FATIGUE (73% confidence)**

## What It Does
Pre-configured session layouts for common development patterns. One command to spawn a session optimized for code review, refactoring, greenfield development, etc.

## Why This Matters
- Currently: Users must decide agent counts and roles each time
- After: Common patterns are one command away
- Especially valuable for newcomers learning optimal configurations

## Built-in Templates
ntm spawn myproject --template=code-review
ntm spawn myproject --template=refactor
ntm spawn myproject --template=greenfield
ntm spawn myproject --template=bug-hunt
ntm spawn myproject --template=documentation

## Template Definition Format (templates.toml)
[[templates]]
name = 'code-review'
description = 'Optimized for reviewing PRs and providing feedback'
agents = { cc = 2, cod = 1 }
personas = ['reviewer', 'implementer', 'tester']
initial_prompts = [
    { target = 'cc', message = 'You are a code reviewer...' },
    { target = 'cod', message = 'You will implement fixes...' }
]

## Success Criteria

1. Built-in templates for 5 common workflows
2. Custom template definition via TOML files
3. ntm spawn --template NAME works correctly
4. ntm templates list shows available templates
5. Templates include agent configs and prompts
6. Templates stored in config directory
7. Unit tests for template parsing (80% coverage)
8. E2E tests verify template-based spawning","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:27:22.539340543Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T00:25:58.110146993Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2b35","depends_on_id":"bd-38gl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2b3fg","title":"rust_proxy: Implement proxy daemon detection and status check","description":"Implement proxy daemon detection and status checking.

## Detection Logic

1. Check PATH for rust_proxy binary
2. Check if daemon process running
3. Query status endpoint for health

## Status Response

```json
{
  "running": true,
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "uptime_seconds": 86400,
  "routes": 2,
  "errors": 0
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Binary detection in PATH works
2. Daemon process detection works
3. Status endpoint query works
4. Graceful handling when daemon not running
5. Version comparison for compatibility
6. Unit tests for detection logic","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:44:38.761797314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:06:08.527121610Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2b3fg","depends_on_id":"bd-2zkfw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-fj9f","title":"Task: Implement contextual command suggestions","description":"## Overview
Provide intelligent, context-aware command suggestions based on current NTM state.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/cli/suggestions/contextual.go:
   - SuggestNextCommand(state State) Suggestion
   - State struct capturing current NTM status
   - Suggestion struct with command, reason, example

2. Context detection:
   - No sessions running -> suggest spawn
   - Sessions running, none busy -> suggest send
   - Sessions running, all busy -> suggest status or dashboard
   - Recent errors -> suggest tail or logs
   - Many sessions -> suggest dashboard
   - Beads available -> suggest assign or swarm

3. Suggestion display:
   - Show at end of command output (non-intrusive)
   - Format: 'Next: ntm send myproject --msg="Start task"'
   - Make dismissible via config (suggestions_enabled: false)

4. Integration points:
   - After ntm status: suggest send if agents idle
   - After ntm spawn: suggest send
   - After ntm send: suggest status or tail
   - After error: suggest relevant recovery command

## Implementation Notes
- Keep suggestions brief (one line)
- Don't show suggestion if user seems to know what they're doing
- Rate limit to avoid being annoying

## Acceptance Criteria
- Suggestions appear in appropriate contexts
- Suggestions are actionable and relevant
- Can be disabled via config
- Unit tests for each context scenario","notes":"Optional, non-intrusive suggestions. Do NOT depend on tiers or usage tracking.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonSparrow","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:25:23.670754891Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:04:47.047170376Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T05:04:47.046000212Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-fj9f","depends_on_id":"bd-vcmnv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-fv2hy","title":"Task: Implement real-time synthesis streaming","description":"## Implementation
Add streaming synthesis without altering non-stream behavior:

1. Add `SynthesisChunk` + stream method in existing synthesis code (prefer extending `internal/ensemble/synthesis.go`; only add a new file if unavoidable):
   - `StreamSynthesize(ctx, inputs) (<-chan SynthesisChunk, <-chan error)`
   - Chunk fields: type, content, mode_id, index, timestamp

2. Emission rules:
   - Deterministic ordering (monotonic index)
   - Progress/status chunks at key milestones
   - Backpressure-safe channel usage

3. CLI:
   - `ntm ensemble synthesize --stream` (human-readable incremental output)
   - `--stream --json` emits JSONL events + final summary
   - Ctrl+C cancels, writes checkpoint, prints resume command

4. Checkpoint integration:
   - On cancel/error, write partial checkpoint (bd-3didt)
   - On resume, continue emitting chunks from last index

## Logging
- Log stream start/end, chunk_count, time_to_first_chunk, cancel reason

## Tests
- Unit: chunk ordering + deterministic emission
- Integration: stream + synthesis fixture
- E2E: CLI streaming output and resume path

## Dependencies
- Synthesis System (bd-2qwm8)
- Synthesis strategies (bd-2ztw9)
- Partial recovery (bd-3didt)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:42:12.555031671Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:55:25.570334908Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:55:25.570260119Z","close_reason":"Completed (streaming synth + checkpoints + E2E tests)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-fv2hy","depends_on_id":"bd-2nnsg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-fv2hy","depends_on_id":"bd-2qwm8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-fv2hy","depends_on_id":"bd-2ztw9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-fv2hy","depends_on_id":"bd-3didt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-fy2cm","title":"Implement --dry-run and explicit allocation","description":"Implement --dry-run and explicit allocation modes.

## --dry-run Mode

Show allocation plan without executing:
```json
{
  "dry_run": true,
  "planned_assignments": [
    {
      "pane": 2,
      "would_assign": "bd-92e1",
      "reason": "highest_unblocks (9)"
    }
  ]
}
```

Useful for:
- Reviewing before executing
- Integration testing
- What-if analysis

## Explicit Allocation (--allocation)

Accept JSON mapping of pane to bead:
```bash
ntm --robot-bulk-assign myproject --allocation='{"2":"bd-92e1","3":"bd-55pm"}'
```

Bypasses bv triage, uses exactly what user specifies.

## --skip-panes Flag

Exclude specific panes from allocation:
```bash
ntm --robot-bulk-assign myproject --from-bv --skip-panes=4,10
```

Useful when some panes are:
- Already working on something
- Rate limited (leave them alone)
- Reserved for manual control","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:31:40.064506799Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T02:02:19.677061450Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T02:02:19.677020303Z","close_reason":"Implemented --dry-run and --allocation flags for bulk assign","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-fy2cm","depends_on_id":"bd-t9qp4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-vcmnv","title":"EPIC: CLI Help Simplification (minimal/full + optional suggestions)","description":"## Goal
Reduce cognitive overload without gating functionality.

## Decision
- Replace tiered/proficiency gating with a **simple help verbosity switch**.
- Keep **contextual suggestions** as an optional, non-intrusive aid.

## Scope
- `ntm help --minimal` and `ntm help --full` (or equivalent)
- Optional suggestions module (disable-able via config)
- No changes to command availability

## Non-Goals
- No tiers, promotions, or usage tracking.

## Success Criteria
- Minimal help is concise and stable
- Full help remains complete
- Suggestions are helpful but unobtrusive","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-ve9hj","title":"Global spawn scheduler with paced pane/agent creation","description":"Implement a global spawn scheduler that serializes and paces all pane + agent creation (tmux new-session/split + agent process launch). Provide queueing, max concurrent spawns, and a rate limiter (token/leaky bucket with optional burst), preserving request ordering and fairness across sessions without removing any existing behaviors. Tests: comprehensive unit tests for limiter math, queue fairness/ordering, starvation prevention, burst handling, and scheduler pause/resume; e2e script that spawns N panes and validates pacing via timestamps, absence of EAGAIN, and detailed spawn logs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-24T03:19:38.539134018Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T09:44:00.476913992Z","closed_at":"2026-01-24T09:44:00.476356702Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["resource-limits","stability"]}

 {"id":"bd-w254z","title":"AccountRotator: Detect current account state","description":"## Purpose
Detect when an agent hits usage limits by monitoring pane output and trigger account rotation through caam integration.

## Implementation Details

```go
// AccountRotator manages caam-based account switching on limit detection
type AccountRotator struct {
    caamPath       string           // Path to caam binary
    limitDetector  *LimitDetector
    rotationState  map[string]*RotationState // pane -> state
    mu             sync.RWMutex
    logger         *slog.Logger
}

type RotationState struct {
    CurrentAccount   string
    PreviousAccounts []string
    RotationCount    int
    LastRotation     time.Time
}

// OnLimitHit is called when LimitDetector detects a rate limit
func (ar *AccountRotator) OnLimitHit(event LimitHitEvent) error {
    ar.mu.Lock()
    defer ar.mu.Unlock()
    
    state := ar.getOrCreateState(event.SessionPane)
    
    ar.logger.Info("limit hit detected, initiating account rotation",
        "pane", event.SessionPane,
        "agent_type", event.AgentType,
        "pattern", event.Pattern,
        "current_account", state.CurrentAccount,
        "rotation_count", state.RotationCount)
    
    // Get next available account from caam
    newAccount, err := ar.getNextAccount(event.AgentType)
    if err != nil {
        ar.logger.Error("failed to get next account",
            "agent_type", event.AgentType,
            "error", err)
        return fmt.Errorf("get next account: %w", err)
    }
    
    // Track rotation
    state.PreviousAccounts = append(state.PreviousAccounts, state.CurrentAccount)
    state.CurrentAccount = newAccount
    state.RotationCount++
    state.LastRotation = time.Now()
    
    ar.logger.Info("account rotation prepared",
        "pane", event.SessionPane,
        "old_account", state.PreviousAccounts[len(state.PreviousAccounts)-1],
        "new_account", newAccount,
        "total_rotations", state.RotationCount)
    
    return nil
}
```

## Integration with caam
- Invoke `caam switch <provider> --next` to get next available account
- Parse caam JSON output for account details
- Handle case when no accounts are available

## Logging Requirements
- Log limit detection trigger with full event context
- Log current account before rotation
- Log new account after rotation
- Track total rotation count per pane
- Error logging with caam command details

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detect limit hits from LimitDetector events
- [ ] Invoke caam CLI for account switching
- [ ] Track rotation history per pane
- [ ] Handle caam errors gracefully
- [ ] Prevent rapid rotation loops (cooldown)
- [ ] Structured logging at each rotation step","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:01:42.400024504Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T22:47:34.334499044Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T22:47:34.334476261Z","close_reason":"Implemented per-pane RotationState tracking, OnLimitHit handler with cooldown logic, and comprehensive tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-w254z","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-w254z","depends_on_id":"bd-3rngh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-w56c","title":"Fix layout breakpoint transitions causing UI jumps","description":"## Problem
When terminal width crosses layout breakpoints (120, 200, 240 cols), UI suddenly reorganizes causing jarring visual jumps.

## Code Review Findings (2026-01-19)

### Confirmed Issue: No Hysteresis or Debounce

**dashboard.go:668-684** - Direct tier calculation on resize:
```go
func (m *Model) handleWindowSize(msg tea.WindowSizeMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
    m.width = width
    m.height = height
    m.tier = layout.TierForWidth(width)  // Immediate tier switch
}
```

**layout.go:54-67** - No hysteresis in tier detection - exact thresholds, no buffer

### Root Cause
- Tier changes happen instantly at exact pixel boundaries
- No debounce on resize events
- No smooth transition between layouts

## Fix Implementation

### 1. Add Hysteresis to Tier Detection
```go
// layout.go
const HysteresisBuffer = 5 // cols

func TierForWidthWithHysteresis(width int, currentTier Tier) Tier {
    newTier := TierForWidth(width)
    if newTier == currentTier {
        return currentTier
    }
    // Only switch if we've moved 5+ cols past threshold
    // ...implementation details...
}
```

### 2. Add Debounce to Resize Handler
Delay applying resize by 100ms to batch rapid events.

## Unit Tests Required
```go
func TestTierHysteresis(t *testing.T) {
    // Start at narrow, resize to just past threshold
    // At 120 exactly, should still be narrow (hysteresis)
    // At 125, should switch to split
    // At 118, should stay split (prevents switch back)
}
```

## E2E Test (VHS)
```tape
# layout_transition_test.tape
Set Width 250
Type "ntm dashboard --demo-data"
Sleep 2s
# Slowly resize down through breakpoints
Set Width 130
Sleep 1s
# Verify smooth transitions
```

## Verification
- [ ] Slowly resize through breakpoints - no sudden jumps
- [ ] Rapid resize - debounce prevents flickering
- [ ] Hysteresis prevents tier oscillation at exact thresholds","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:10:30.167252379Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T09:17:35.076312014Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:17:31.850130704Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-w56c","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-w56c","depends_on_id":"bd-3v69","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":82,"issue_id":"bd-w56c","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented hysteresis to prevent UI jumps at breakpoint boundaries:

1. Added HysteresisBuffer constant (5 cols) to layout.go
2. Added TierForWidthWithHysteresis() function that prevents tier switches until width passes threshold + buffer
3. Added thresholdForTier() helper function
4. Updated dashboard.go handleWindowSize() and WindowSizeMsg handler to use hysteresis
5. Created comprehensive test suite in layout_test.go with 40+ test cases
6. Updated dashboard_resize_test.go with hysteresis-aware tests

The fix prevents flickering when terminal width hovers near breakpoints (120, 200, 240, 320). All tests pass.","created_at":"2026-01-19T09:17:35Z"}]}
